title: Hack@DAC 2021: AES CTR counter never increments
hook_30s: AES in counter mode requires a counter that increments every block. If it doesn't, your cipher becomes a one-time-pad with a fixed keystream — a known-plaintext attack recovers your secrets. This bug was inserted in a Hack@DAC SoC. ChipGPT finds it in 8 seconds.

steps.agent: I'll check this RTL against the NIST SP 800-38A CTR mode spec. Let me start by extracting spec facts and parsing the RTL.
Reading the spec; extracting the canonicality rule.
Spec rule found at lines 11 (successive counter blocks must differ). Let me locate the next-state logic.

In aes_ctr_counter, the next-state logic at line 18 assigns ctr_d = ctr_q unconditionally. The counter register is therefore held at its reset/init value forever; the spec-correct increment (ctr_d = ctr_q + 1 when mode_ctr_i && block_done_i) is commented out and never applied. block_done_i and mode_ctr_i are read into the module but never affect ctr_d.

artifact.explanation: The spec states the successive counter blocks must differ, each derived by incrementing the previous. The assertion requires that, after a block completes in CTR mode, the counter output differs from its previous value. Formal BMC returned a counterexample (CEX) on the buggy RTL, proving the property is violated because ctr_d = ctr_q holds the counter.
artifact.result: SymbiYosys (z3): counterexample found — bug proven formally

steps.agent: Running the directed test in the simulator.

artifact.name: tb_ctr_advances_on_block_done
artifact.stimulus: Reset with ctr_init_i=0x42, mode_ctr_i=1. Capture T_1 after reset. Pulse block_done_i for one cycle, four times, sampling ctr_o after each completed block; compare against the first block.
artifact.result_on_buggy: FAIL — ctr_o stays 0x42 for all 4 blocks; 'counter did not advance; keystream reused' printed each time.
artifact.result_on_fixed: PASS — with conditional increment, ctr_o advances to 0x43,0x44,... and never equals T_1.

artifact.title: AES CTR counter never increments — keystream reuse across blocks
artifact.root_cause: In aes_ctr_counter, the next-state logic at line 18 assigns ctr_d = ctr_q unconditionally. The counter register is therefore held at its reset/init value forever; the spec-correct increment (ctr_d = ctr_q + 1 when mode_ctr_i && block_done_i) is commented out and never applied. block_done_i and mode_ctr_i are read into the module but never affect ctr_d.
artifact.fix: Replace line 18 with conditional increment: ctr_d = ctr_q; if (mode_ctr_i && block_done_i) ctr_d = ctr_q + 128'd1; so each successive counter block differs from the previous one as NIST SP 800-38A §6.5 requires.
artifact.impact: Every block of a CTR-mode message is enciphered with E(K, ctr) for the SAME ctr, producing an identical keystream block T_1 = T_2 = ... = T_n. CTR degenerates to a fixed-keystream one-time-pad: C_j = P_j XOR KS, so C_a XOR C_b = P_a XOR P_b leaks plaintext, and a single known plaintext block recovers the keystream and decrypts the entire message. Catastrophic confidentiality loss.

artifact.coverage_status: Proven both ways: formal CEX (symbiyosys+z3, depth 4) and directed sim FAIL (iverilog).

proof.note: Temporal property ($past) — reproduced by a 2-block directed test. Open-source formal returns UNKNOWN; a formal proof needs commercial SVA (JasperGold / VC Formal).
